title: Load Combinations
description: Buildings and structures shall be designed for minimum live, dead, and wind loads. Critical for structural calculations in remodels involving roof loads, floor loads, or wall removal.

title: Exterior Walls - Fire Resistance
description: Exterior walls must have fire resistance ratings based on proximity to property lines and other buildings. Applies when adding windows, doors, or altering exterior walls.

title: Garage Opening Protection
description: Doors between garage and dwelling must be self-closing 20-minute fire-rated assemblies. Any penetration must be caulked or sealed.

title: Fire Protection of Floors
description: Floor assemblies separating dwelling units or between a dwelling and attached garage must be protected with 5/8" Type X gypsum board.

title: Habitable Rooms - Light and Ventilation
description: Habitable rooms must have minimum glazing area of 8% of floor area and openable area of 4% for natural ventilation. Key for remodels converting basements or adding rooms.

title: Bathroom Ventilation
description: Bathrooms must have an exhaust fan vented to the outside (minimum 50 CFM intermittent or 20 CFM continuous). Required in all bathroom remodels.

title: Mechanical Ventilation
description: When natural ventilation is insufficient, mechanical ventilation must be provided per ASHRAE 62.2. Required for tight homes and additions.

title: Minimum Room Area
description: Habitable rooms must have a minimum of 70 sq ft of floor area. Affects remodel layouts and room reconfigurations.

title: Minimum Ceiling Height
description: Habitable rooms and hallways must have a minimum ceiling height of 7 ft. Basements, bathrooms, and laundry rooms have exceptions. Critical for basement finishing and remodels.

title: Sanitation - Toilet Facilities
description: Each dwelling must have at least one water closet, lavatory, bathtub or shower. Required for any ADU or addition.

title: Bathroom Clearances
description: Minimum clearances in front of toilet (21 inches), lavatory (21 inches), and tub/shower entry. Critical for bathroom remodel layout planning.

title: Hazardous Glazing Locations
description: Glazing in hazardous locations (doors, side panels, low windows, showers) must be safety tempered or laminated. Required in all remodel work near glazing.

title: Glazing in Guards and Railings
description: Glazing used in guards, railings, or balustrades must be safety glass and withstand structural loads. Applies to deck remodels and stair upgrades.

title: Garage Fire Separation
description: Garages must be separated from dwelling and interior rooms by 1/2" gypsum board on garage side. Floor-ceiling assembly above garage must be 5/8" Type X.

title: Emergency Escape and Rescue Openings
description: Basements and sleeping rooms must have an emergency escape window with minimum opening of 5.7 sq ft, width >= 20", height >= 24", and sill height <= 44". Critical for basement remodels and bedroom additions.

title: Stairways
description: Stair risers max 7-3/4", treads min 10", handrails required on at least one side. Headroom min 6'8". Applies to any remodel involving stair modification or addition.

title: Stair Handrails
description: Handrails required on at least one side of stairs with 4+ risers. Must be between 34"-38" measured from stair nosing. Must allow continuous grip.

title: Guards - Required Height
description: Guards required where drop exceeds 30". Minimum height 36" for residential. Balusters must not allow passage of 4" sphere. Critical for decks, balconies, and stair remodels.

title: Automatic Fire Sprinkler Systems
description: New one- and two-family dwellings require automatic fire sprinklers. Remodels exceeding 50% of building value may trigger full compliance. Major cost consideration.

title: Smoke Alarms
description: Smoke alarms required in each sleeping room, outside each sleeping area, and on every level including basements. Must be hardwired with battery backup and interconnected. Required in any remodel.

title: Carbon Monoxide Alarms
description: CO alarms required outside sleeping areas in dwellings with attached garages or fuel-burning appliances. Must be hardwired with battery backup. Required in most remodels.

title: Foam Plastic Insulation Protection
description: Foam plastic insulation must be separated from interior spaces by minimum 1/2" gypsum board or equivalent thermal barrier. Affects insulation choices in remodels.

title: Penetrations of Fire-Resistant Assemblies
description: Penetrations through fire-rated assemblies must be protected with approved firestop materials. Applies when running new wiring, ducts, or pipes.

title: Protection Against Termites
description: In CA (moderate to heavy termite probability), wood framing must be minimum 18" above exposed earth. Requires termite treatment in many areas. Critical for foundation work.

title: Site Address
description: Address numbers must be clearly visible from the street, minimum 4" height, contrasting color. Required for any new construction or ADU.

title: Flood-Resistant Construction
description: In flood hazard areas, buildings must be constructed with flood-resistant materials and elevated to required flood levels. Critical for coastal and floodplain projects.

title: Storm Shelters
description: In tornado-prone areas, storm shelters may be required for certain occupancies. Less common in CA but relevant in some regions.

title: Solar Energy Systems
description: California requires solar photovoltaic systems on new dwellings (per CALGreen). Roof must accommodate solar access. Affects roof framing and orientation planning.

title: Mezzanines
description: Mezzanines must comply with minimum area, ceiling height, and egress requirements. Relevant for loft additions and open-plan remodels.

title: Swimming Pools, Spas, and Hot Tubs
description: Barrier requirements for pools and spas: minimum 4 ft high, self-closing gates, no climbable features. Critical for pool/spa addition projects.

title: Emergency Planning and Preparedness
description: Requirements for emergency access pathways and defensible space in wildfire-prone areas. Critical for CA wildfire zone construction.

title: Cannabis Production
description: Requirements for dwelling units used for cannabis production, including ventilation, electrical, and fire safety. Relevant for certain CA projects.

title: Foundations - General
description: All exterior walls must be supported on foundations. Minimum foundation depth 12" below undisturbed soil. Affects additions and foundation repairs.

title: Minimum Footing Size
description: Minimum footing width based on number of stories and soil bearing capacity. Single story: 12" wide, two story: 15" wide. Critical for additions.

title: Foundation Walls
description: Concrete and masonry foundation walls must comply with minimum thickness and reinforcement requirements based on height and lateral load. Key for basement remodels.

title: Wood Deck Construction
description: Decks must be designed for live load of 40 psf and dead load of 10 psf. Connections to building must resist lateral forces. Critical for all deck remodels and additions.

title: Wall Framing
description: Stud spacing, height, and bracing requirements. Maximum stud spacing 24" OC. Minimum 2x4 studs for exterior walls. Affects framing in any remodel.

title: Headers
description: Headers required over openings in load-bearing walls. Size based on span and load. Critical for window/door additions and wall removal projects.

title: Gypsum Board Installation
description: Gypsum board must be installed per manufacturer instructions. Fastener spacing, joint treatment, and fire-rated assembly requirements. Applies to all drywall work.

title: Roof Framing
description: Roof rafter and ceiling joist sizing based on span, spacing, and load. Minimum 2x6 rafters at 24" OC for most spans. Affects roof modifications and additions.

title: Roof Covering
description: Roof covering must meet fire classification based on building location. Class A required in wildfire zones. Applies to all re-roofing projects.

title: Electric Vehicle Charging
description: New dwellings must have EV charging infrastructure (panel capacity, conduit to parking). Required for all new construction and major remodels.

title: Water-Cooling Towers
description: Requirements for water conservation in cooling towers. Relevant for commercial HVAC projects.

title: Water Fountains
description: Water fountains must be bottle-filling type. Relevant for commercial remodels and tenant improvements.

title: Drought-Tolerant Landscaping
description: Requires use of drought-tolerant plants and efficient irrigation systems. Affects landscaping for new construction and major renovations.

title: Construction Waste Management
description: Minimum 50% diversion of construction waste from landfills. Requires waste management plan. Critical for project bidding and cost estimation.

title: Low-VOC Materials
description: Paints, adhesives, sealants, and flooring must meet low-VOC content limits. Required for all interior finish work. Affects material selection and costs.

title: Indoor Moisture Control
description: Vapor retarders and moisture control measures to prevent mold. Required in all construction. Affects insulation and wall assembly choices.

title: Commissioning
description: Non-residential buildings over 10,000 sq ft require mechanical system commissioning. Relevant for commercial projects.

title: Accessibility - Residential (Housing)
description: Adaptable housing requirements for multi-family dwellings. Includes accessible routes, kitchen/bathroom adaptation provisions. Critical for multi-family projects.

title: Accessibility - Public Accommodations
description: Accessibility requirements for public buildings and commercial spaces. Includes accessible routes, bathrooms, parking. Required for all commercial remodels.

title: Exterior Wildfire Exposure
description: Materials and construction methods for buildings in wildfire zones. Includes ignition-resistant roofing, siding, decking, and venting. Critical for CA wildfire areas.

title: Fire Sprinkler Systems - Where Required
description: Fire sprinkler requirements based on occupancy group, building area, and height. Major cost consideration for commercial and multi-family projects.

title: Classification of Work
description: Defines alterations as Level 1 (finish only), Level 2 (reconfiguration), or Level 3 (extensive structural). Determines code compliance path for remodel projects.

title: Repairs - General
description: Repairs must not make the building less compliant than before. Applies to all repair work in existing buildings.

title: Level 1 Alterations
description: Finish-only alterations (paint, flooring, cabinets) require compliance for the work itself but not full building upgrade. Least restrictive path.

title: Level 2 Alterations
description: Reconfiguration of space (wall moves, room changes) requires compliance for the altered area plus 20% of the building value in upgrades. Common for tenant improvements.

title: Level 3 Alterations
description: Extensive structural alterations trigger full building compliance for seismic, fire, and accessibility. Major cost implications.

title: Water Supply and Distribution
description: Minimum pipe sizes, fixture counts, and backflow prevention requirements. Critical for plumbing remodels and additions.

title: Sanitary Drainage
description: Drain pipe sizing, slope requirements (1/4" per ft for 3" and smaller), and venting requirements. Critical for any added fixtures.

title: Vents
description: Vent sizing, distance from trap, and vent termination requirements. Critical when adding plumbing fixtures in remodels.

title: General Regulations - Mechanical
description: Heating and cooling load calculations, equipment sizing, and duct design requirements. Required for HVAC replacement or addition.

title: Ventilation Air
description: Minimum ventilation requirements for occupied spaces. Outdoor air rates per ASHRAE 62. Applies to all occupied spaces.

title: Dwelling Unit Receptacle Outlets
description: Receptacle spacing requirements: no point along wall more than 6 ft from an outlet. Required spacing in kitchens, bathrooms, and outdoors. Affects all electrical work.

title: GFCI Protection
description: GFCI outlets required in bathrooms, kitchens, garages, outdoors, basements, and within 6 ft of sinks. Required for all new/replaced outlets in these areas.

title: AFCI Protection
description: AFCI breakers required for all 15A and 20A branch circuits supplying sleeping areas and most habitable rooms. Required for new circuits in remodels.

title: Service Disconnect
description: Service disconnect must be at a readily accessible location. Main panel clearance requirements. Important for panel upgrade projects.

title: Equipment Grounding
description: All raceways, cable trays, and enclosures must be mechanically and electrically continuous. Required for all electrical installations.

title: Protection of Conductors
description: Wiring must be protected from physical damage. Nail plates required where wires pass through studs within 1-1/4" of edge. Required for all rough-in work.

title: Outlet Boxes
description: Minimum box fill calculations and support requirements. Box must be sized for number of conductors. Applies to all electrical box installations.

title: Receptacles in Wet Locations
description: Outdoor and wet-location receptacles must have weather-resistant covers. Required for all exterior outlet installations.

title: Luminaires in Clothes Closets
description: Clearance requirements for light fixtures in closets based on storage area. Surface-mounted LED must be 12" from storage. Important for all closet projects.

title: Appliances - Disconnecting Means
description: Built-in appliances must have a means of disconnect within sight or be capable of being locked off. Required for kitchen remodels.

title: Dishwasher Connection
description: Dishwashers must be connected with a cord and plug or a dedicated disconnect. Required for kitchen remodels.

title: Manufactured Buildings and Structures
description: Requirements for manufactured homes and ADUs. Relevant for prefab ADU installations.

title: Electric Vehicle Charging
description: EV charging circuit requirements. Level 2 charging requires dedicated 40A or 50A circuit. Required for new EV charger installations.

title: Solar Photovoltaic Systems
description: PV system installation requirements including rapid shutdown, disconnect, and labeling. Required for solar installations.

title: Emergency Systems
description: Emergency lighting and power requirements for commercial buildings. Required for egress paths in commercial remodels.
